title: draba incana subsp. thomasii
intro: Draba incana subsp. thomasii, also known as Thomas's whitlowgrass, is a perennial plant species in the mustard family (Brassicaceae). It is used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The plant's leaves and stems are used to make herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can help alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and sore throats, as well as promote overall health and well-being. Additionally, the plant has been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and stomach cramps, due to its carminative and anti-inflammatory properties.
